strengths: Experience building ETL pipelines and data models
Solid visualization and dashboarding (Power BI, Tableau)
weaknesses: Limited explicit machine learning library usage
No documented production ML model deployment
Shorter overall data science tenure (~2–3 years)
No explicit A/B testing or causal inference experience
improvement_bullets: Developed predictive models for customer segmentation using scikit-learn, applying machine learning techniques to identify high-value borrowers and reduce default risk in loan portfolios.
Engineered end-to-end ML pipelines to process 2M+ row datasets, focusing on feature engineering and model tuning to optimize algorithm performance and predictive accuracy.
Designed and executed A/B testing frameworks for marketing campaigns, utilizing statistical analysis to evaluate conversion rate lift and inform product growth strategies.
Optimized data workflows for large-scale datasets, ensuring robust data profiling and validation practices to maintain data integrity in production-level code environments.
Partnered with product teams to translate complex model outputs into actionable product recommendations, using data storytelling to communicate experiment results and drive feature adoption.
cover_letter: Dear Hiring Manager, I am excited to submit my application for the Paze Product Data Scientist II opportunity with Early Warning Services. I am interested in this opportunity because of Early Warning's critical role in securing the U.S. financial system through innovative products like Zelle and Paze. What interests me about working for Early Warning is its commitment to both scale and security, which inspires me because it presents the exact type of high-stakes, data-rich environment where I excel. I believe my expertise in building scalable ETL pipelines and my proficiency in statistical modeling would make me a strong candidate for this role. During my professional career, I have approached problems with a focus on translating raw data into clear, actionable business strategy. In fact, while at Shine Masters, I implemented time series models to identify customer segments, which directly informed targeted marketing initiatives that drove a 300% increase in new customer acquisition over 12 months. Ultimately, I know that the Paze Product Data Scientist II role at Early Warning would allow me to bring my experience in predictive modeling to optimize the Paze product experience. Additionally, my role as a Data Analyst at Shine Masters enabled me to develop my feature engineering skills. I automated complex intake workflows and redesigned reporting, which reduced data processing time by 40% and enabled executive leadership to make informed, data-driven decisions at scale. I’m excited by the opportunity to use my machine learning and experimentation skills in working on product performance analysis at Early Warning. I know that my background in Python and SQL and my passion for data storytelling align with what it takes to succeed at the Paze Product Data Scientist II role. I would love the opportunity to discuss the role in more detail. Thank you for your time and consideration. I look forward to hearing from you soon. Warm regards, Justin Miller
